title: Benefits
description: Practice learning of soft vs hard benefits.

assessment_items.question_content:
Job benefits can be divided into two categories: soft and hard benefits.

**Which of these is an example of a soft benefit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Job benefits can be divided into two categories: soft and hard benefits.

**Which of the following is an example of a soft benefit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Job benefits can be divided into two categories: soft and hard benefits.

**Which one below is a soft benefit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

We can split job benefits into two groups: hard benefits and soft benefits.

**Which of the following is a hard benefit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

We can split job benefits into two groups: hard benefits and soft benefits.

**From the options below, which one is an example of a hard benefit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

We can split job benefits into two groups: hard benefits and soft benefits.

**Which benefit below is considered a hard benefit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

In addition to pay, most jobs offer some type of benefits, typically split into hard and soft benefits.

**What is a soft job benefit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It is the amount of money you earn from work.
It's the part of a job that makes it better, and more enjoyable.
It is the extra things your employer pays for that benefit you, like a gym membership.

assessment_items.question_content:
In addition to pay, most jobs offer some type of benefits, typically split into hard and soft benefits.

**What is a hard job benefit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A hard job benefit is something that makes your job nicer or easier, but isn't about money.
A hard job benefit is when your job helps you pay for things.
A hard job benefit is about making friends at work.
A hard job benefit can include developing your time management skills.

assessment_items.question_content:
In addition to pay, most jobs offer some type of benefits, typically split into hard and soft benefits.

**What is a hard job benefit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A hard job benefit is something that improves your job but doesn't involve money.
A hard job benefit is about getting money or financial assistance from your job.
A hard job benefit is about enjoying your time at work.
A hard benefit of a job can include helping you manage your daily life responsibilities.

assessment_items.question_content:
Imagine you are a parent juggling two kids and their before and after-school activities.

**Which soft job benefit would be most useful for you to manage these activities?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: an office near a coffee shop

assessment_items.question_content:
You've recently experienced some health problems and need frequent doctor's visits. 

**Which benefit would help you manage these expenses?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the option to work from home

assessment_items.question_content:
You're traveling a lot for work and sometimes find it challenging to maintain a healthy lifestyle.

**Which benefit would support you best in this situation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a wellness program that offers diet and exercise advice
gift cards to fast food restaurants.
